name: Steve Jordan
abstract: Steve Philip Jordan (January 15, 1919 – September 13, 1993) was an American jazz guitarist.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
Midnight Performance: Jazz Concert by Benny Goodman and Louis Armstrong
All-Star Jazz Concert and Recording Session

about_quotes.speaker: Chris Parker
about_quotes.evidence: I spotted Steve Jordan in the audience standing on the floor. And I said, “Steve, come on up and play.” I gave him the part that I was playing... And Steve is playing shaker and tambourine. And we’re looking at each other during the thing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chris Parker interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 5, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: So -- Steve Jordan is a very good guitarist. He's from one of the big bands in the old era. He's a guitarist and he sings. But he's very good and he's got a very good sense of rhythm.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Chez
about_quotes.evidence: I did it for 17 years... I think it was Steve Jordan then Anton... These guys don’t want to rehearse... So our rehearsals ran seven to nine minutes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Chez interviewed by Monk Rowe, New Hartford, New York, February 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: Again, we got someone else, Steve Jordan, the famous drummer for everyone... he’s played with them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
